docs.title: AddressTools
docs.text: Welcome to AddressTools documentation. AddressTools is a comprehensive address verification and validation solution for Salesforce that ensures your address data is accurate, standardised, and up to date.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: AddressTools enhances your territory management, reduces delivery failures, simplifies reporting, and improves data quality through global address verification and validation. Whether you need to verify addresses at point of entry or clean existing data, AddressTools has you covered.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Global Address Verification: Verify addresses against postal authorities worldwide (USPS, Canada Post, Royal Mail, etc.)
  • Geocoding: Add latitude and longitude coordinates to verified addresses for location-based workflows
  • Advanced Validation Rules: Flexible country, state, and ZIP code validation rules
  • Country & State Standardisation: Standardise country and state values to your preferred format
  • Flow Component: Native Salesforce Flow component for address verification in workflows
  • Interactive Data Entry: ZIP code lookup, country/state suggestions, and autocomplete features
  • US County Data: Autopopulate accurate county values using ZIP+4 codes
  • Enhanced Address Editor: Lightning component for viewing and editing addresses
  • Custom Objects Support: Verify addresses on custom objects and fields
  • Per-Profile Configuration: Customise settings for different users and profiles

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• User Guide - Complete setup and usage instructions
  • Getting Started - Quick deployment guide
  • Flow Component Guide - Integrate address verification in Salesforce Flows
  • Validation Rules - Configure custom validation rules
  • Geocoding Guide - Add coordinates to addresses
  • FAQ - Common questions and answers
  • Pricing - Licensing information

docs.title: Pricing
docs.text:
Starting at $2,400 USD/year (org with 50 or fewer users)

  • Global premise-level address verification: Starting at $1,000 USD
  • Geocoding: Available as premium add-on with verification credits
  • Not-for-profit discounts available

View full pricing →

docs.title: Free Features
docs.text:
• Country and state datasets
  • Country/state suggestions
  • Basic validation rules
  • Custom profile configuration

docs.title: Premium Features
docs.text:
• Premise-level address verification
  • PowerSearch typeahead
  • Interactive batch verification
  • Scheduled batch jobs
  • Geocoding integration
  • US county data
  • Custom objects and fields
  • Advanced validation rules
  • Flow component integration

docs.title: Common Use Cases
docs.text:
• Data Entry Validation: Ensure clean data at point of entry with interactive suggestions
  • Batch Verification: Verify and standardise existing address data
  • Territory Management: Use geocoding for location-based territory assignments
  • International Compliance: Standardise addresses across global offices
  • Delivery Optimization: Reduce delivery failures with verified, standardised addresses

docs.title: Benefits
docs.text: ✓ Improved data quality and reporting accuracy ✓ Reduced delivery failures and costs ✓ Better territory management with geocoding ✓ Compliance with international address standards ✓ Fast implementation with AppExchange 14-day trial

docs.title: PhoneTools
docs.text:
(AI generated MD for prototyping purposes)

Welcome to PhoneTools documentation. PhoneTools is a quick-to-deploy TPS and CTPS screening solution for Salesforce that helps businesses stay compliant with UK data privacy laws when making cold calls.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: PhoneTools screens your Salesforce records against the UK Telephone Preference Service (TPS) and Corporate TPS (CTPS) lists sourced directly from the DMA with daily refreshes. Stay compliant with ICO requirements and mitigate the risk of fines.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• TPS and CTPS Screening: Screen phone numbers against both individual and corporate preference lists
  • Simple Traffic Light System: Instantly view screening status with clear visual indicators
  • Manual and Automated Screening: Use the Screen Phone Numbers button or set up automated batch screenings
  • Compliance Tracking: 28-day screening cycle tracking to remain compliant with ICO requirements
  • Salesforce Native: No need to leave Salesforce to check compliance status
  • Flow Integration: Queue immediate screening jobs to get results in seconds
  • Reporting & Workflows: Use screening status in reports, queues, and custom workflows
  • JSON Output: Leverage configurable Phone Screening Results field for custom development

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• User Guide - Complete setup and usage instructions
  • Getting Started - Quick deployment guide
  • FAQ - Common questions and answers
  • Troubleshooting - Support resources

docs.title: Pricing
docs.text:
Free Tier: Up to 250 records screened monthly Premium: Starting from £1,500/year for up to 10,000 records monthly

View full pricing →

docs.title: Who Should Use PhoneTools?
docs.text:
• UK businesses making cold calls
  • Sales and marketing teams requiring compliance
  • Contact center operations
  • Companies handling phone-based lead generation

docs.title: PhoneTools User Guide
docs.text: This guide will walk you through installing, configuring, and using PhoneTools in your Salesforce organisation.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
1. Getting Started
  2. Installation
  3. Configuration
  4. Screening Operations
  5. Compliance Tracking
  6. Best Practices
  7. Troubleshooting

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: PhoneTools is a Salesforce AppExchange application that screens your contact phone numbers against the UK Telephone Preference Service (TPS) and Corporate TPS (CTPS) lists.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Salesforce organisation (any edition)
  • Administrator access to install applications
  • UK-based phone numbers to screen

docs.title: Step 1: Install from AppExchange
docs.text:
1. Go to Salesforce AppExchange
  2. Search for "PhoneTools"
  3. Click Install
  4. Select your Salesforce organisation
  5. Review permissions and click Install

docs.title: Step 2: Verify Installation
docs.text:
1. In Salesforce, navigate to Setup
  2. Search for Installed Packages
  3. Verify PhoneTools appears in the list

docs.title: Add Screening Fields to Records
docs.text:
1. Go to object layout (e.g., Contacts, Leads)
  2. Add the following fields to your layout:
  3. Phone Screening Status
  4. Phone Screening Last Checked
  5. Phone Screening Next Due
  6. Phone Screening Results (JSON)

docs.title: Set Up Automated Screening
docs.text:
1. Navigate to PhoneTools Settings
  2. Enable Automated Screening
  3. Set your preferred screening schedule
  4. Configure which object types to screen

docs.title: Manual Screening
docs.text:
1. Open a record with a phone number
  2. Click the Screen Phone Numbers button
  3. View results instantly in the Phone Screening Status field:
  4. 🟢 Green: Number is safe to call
  5. 🟡 Amber: Action recommended
  6. 🔴 Red: Do not call - number is registered

docs.title: Batch Screening
docs.text:
Screen multiple records in one operation:

  1. Go to list view for your records
  2. Select multiple records
  3. Click Screen Phone Numbers (batch)
  4. Wait for processing to complete

docs.title: Flow-Based Screening
docs.text:
Use Salesforce Flows to queue immediate screening:

Flow: Screen Phone on Record Update
├─ Trigger: Record is created or updated
├─ Action: Queue Phone Screening Job
├─ Wait: For result
└─ Action: Update record with status

docs.title: Understanding the 28-Day Cycle
docs.text:
ICO regulations require phone numbers to be screened at least every 28 days. PhoneTools tracks this automatically:

  • Phone Screening Last Checked: Date of last screening
  • Phone Screening Next Due: When next screening is required
  • Use these fields in reports and workflows to stay compliant

docs.title: Create a Compliance Report
docs.text:
1. Click + New Report
  2. Select object (Contacts, Leads, etc.)
  3. Add columns:
  4. Name
  5. Phone
  6. Phone Screening Status
  7. Phone Screening Next Due
  8. Filter for records where "Next Due" < TODAY
  9. Save and schedule

docs.title: JSON Output
docs.text:
The Phone Screening Results field contains detailed JSON data for custom development:

{
  "screening_status": "GREEN",
  "tps_status": "NOT_REGISTERED",
  "ctps_status": "NOT_REGISTERED",
  "last_screened": "2026-06-23",
  "next_due": "2026-07-21"
}

docs.title: Build Workflows
docs.text:
Use screening status to automate your processes:

  • Route GREEN numbers to sales queue
  • Alert for AMBER results
  • Block calls for RED numbers
  • Schedule follow-up screening before 28-day limit

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Screen Proactively: Set up automated screening rather than manual
  2. Monitor Compliance: Create dashboard showing compliance status
  3. Plan Ahead: Schedule screenings before the 28-day limit expires
  4. Test First: Verify setup with test records before full rollout
  5. Document Compliance: Keep audit trails of screening activities
  6. Handle Errors Gracefully: Set up error notifications for failed screenings
  7. Optimize Costs: Use formula fields to screen only records that need it

docs.title: Issue: Screening Button Not Appearing
docs.text: Solution: Verify the PhoneTools field has been added to your page layout and user permissions are correct.

docs.title: Issue: Slow Batch Screening
docs.text: Solution: Schedule batch operations during off-peak hours. PhoneTools processes records sequentially to maintain API limits.

docs.title: Issue: Permission Denied
docs.text: Solution: Ensure your Salesforce user profile has permission to access PhoneTools objects and the Screening Phone Numbers custom action.

docs.title: Issue: Incorrect Screening Results
docs.text: Solution: Verify phone numbers include UK country code (+44) or are formatted as UK numbers. DMA updates daily - try screening again.
